title: crateva adansonii subsp. axillaris
intro: Crateva adansonii subsp. axillaris, also known as the Adam's rib or Adam's root, is a plant species of the genus Crateva in the family Capparaceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory problems. Crateva adansonii subsp. axillaris is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
